what_it_does: Walks the project tree, skipping configured top-level paths, and regenerates JSON address and type registries that describe directories, files, parent containment, extensions, byte sizes, and seeded type declarations.
observations: Computes ROOT as three directories above this script and changes the process working directory to that root.
Skips source, reference, .git, and node_modules at the top level, with matching ignore patterns recorded in registry rules.
Creates one address entry per discovered directory and file, using contains as the parent edge.
Classifies directories as root, dir, dir._prefixed, or dir.dotfile based on the basename.
Classifies files by lowercase extension, with (none) used for files without an extension.
Seeds folder type and extension type registry declarations with parser, edge, field, format, and interpretation slots.
Writes registry/address-registry.json and registry/type-registry.json with indent=1.
standouts: The scan output is derived entirely from the live filesystem each run.
The address registry treats folders and files as typed addressable ledger entries.
The type registry is seeded during discovery rather than authored separately in this script.
conventions: Uses simple module constants for skip lists and special filenames.
Uses os.path and os.walk rather than pathlib.
Performs work at import or execution time, with no main guard.
Uses compact one-line conditional returns in dir_type.
Writes JSON directly with open calls inline inside json.dump.
concerns: Changing the working directory at module load can affect callers that import this file.
The script opens output files without context managers.
__pycache__ and similar ignore patterns are recorded in rules but not actively filtered except through the top-level skip set.
Importing the module would regenerate registry files as a side effect.
Filename and directory traversal order are not explicitly sorted, so JSON address order follows os.walk behavior.
notes: A filesystem census script that rebuilds address and type registry seeds from the current tree.
questions: Should ignore_patterns be enforced by the scanner itself beyond the top-level SKIP_TOP filter?
Should special_name entries also seed a special-names registry type section?
Is the script intended to be importable, or only run as a command-line tool?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it rebuilds a derived address-registry skeleton and type-registry seed from the filesystem, though it only enforces part of the recorded ignore policy.
intention_signals: It appears intended to keep a project substrate map synchronized with the live directory tree while preserving slots for later interpretation, parsers, fields, and edge declarations.
novelty: The file treats ordinary filesystem paths as typed ledger addresses and seeds interpretation-oriented registry structure during a basic scan.
contribution: Provides a repeatable filesystem-to-ledger census that contributes address entries, containment edges, file metadata, directory typing, extension typing, and registry rule metadata for later assembly or interpretation.
summary_for_embedding: This is a Python filesystem scanner that regenerates address and type registry JSON files from discovered directories and files under the project root.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to keep a derived substrate ledger aligned with the live tree and to seed type declarations for later registry assembly.

symbols.does: Returns a directory type string based on the first character of a directory name.
symbols.description: This helper classifies directory names into three local categories: underscore-prefixed directories, dotfile-style directories, and ordinary directories. The root directory is handled outside this function as type root.
